items.title: Application root and rendering: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this React: Component-Based Browser Applications misconception? Calling render appends a fresh duplicate DOM tree on every state change.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. React reconciles a root's managed subtree rather than blindly appending a new tree each render.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. React reconciles a root's managed subtree rather than blindly appending a new tree each render.

items.title: Application root and rendering: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Mount one React root and distinguish rendering descriptions from DOM commit work. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating single createRoot mount into an owned container; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.
